explanation: Baking Soda, Yeast, Baking Powder, and Whipped Egg Whites are all primary leavening agents used to make baked goods rise by creating gas. Granulated Sugar adds sweetness and texture but does not directly act as a leavening agent.

category: Pioneering Inventors of the Industrial Revolution
explanation: James Watt, Eli Whitney, Richard Arkwright, and George Stephenson were all instrumental inventors and engineers whose innovations were central to the British Industrial Revolution (primarily 18th-19th centuries), focusing on steam power, textile machinery, and railways. Johannes Gutenberg, while a revolutionary inventor, developed the printing press in the 15th century, predating the Industrial Revolution by several centuries.
"Earth", "Heart", "Rate", and "Tare" are all anagrams of the word "Hart" (an adult male deer). "Hate" is not.
